title: Twig: Aesthetic & Art — Setting, General
excerpt: The setting is effectively the 1920s, and fashion reflects this. In this world, however, someone very like Dr. Frankenstein started off a scientific revolution wherein biology fast outpaced any other technology. The science is tightly

title: Twig: Aesthetic & Art — Lambs
excerpt:
The main characters of the story. The group consists of four characters and their medic, but they’re joined by a sixth member very early on. The Lambs start the story being 13 or so, and are close to 18 by the end of season 2.

With the

title: Twig: Aesthetic & Art — Architecture
excerpt: Tree-like growths run up the sides of buildings and in some places, trees grow out of one corner of the building, merging with early 1900s and victorian architecture. One in three given lots of architecture might have a section that

title: Twig: Aesthetic & Art — Sylvester
excerpt: The protagonist, central character. Sylvester is the roguish personality of the group, the joker (as in the card), and the jack of all trades. Experimented on since infancy, he’s had poisons injected into his brain on a schedule to keep it

title: Twig: Aesthetic & Art — Gordon
excerpt: Gordon is the golden child. The ‘hero’ of the group, and Sylvester’s antithesis in many ways. Everyone likes and looks up to Gordon on the surface, he’s a natural at anything he does, and he’s immediately likable. But his handsome smile

title: Twig: Aesthetic & Art — Helen
excerpt: Helen and Gordon make something of a pair. If Gordon is the ideal boy, then Helen is the ideal girl. Blonde, sweet, dutiful, and attractive, she’s sure to be a knockout when she’s an adult. In reality, she’s not human. She’s a femme fatale

title: Twig: Aesthetic & Art — Jamie
excerpt: Sylvester’s best friend, despite them being an unlikely pair. Jamie isn’t as immersed in the action, as a rule. With longer hair, glasses, and a journal clasped in his arms, he’s the quiet, bookish type. Jamie is the scholar of the group,

title: Twig: Aesthetic & Art — Jessie
excerpt: Appearance: Jessie is Jamie’s older twin, has longer, blonde-brown hair that she grows long into a single braid, that drapes over one shoulder or is bound into a tight loop. She favors horn-rimmed spectacles and silvery tones to her

title: Twig: Aesthetic & Art — Lillian
excerpt: The medic of the group. Lillian joins very close to the story’s outset. She isn’t an experiment, only a student of the Academy meant to be near the Lambs and keep them alive, while not interfering with the generally unassuming image of a

title: Twig: Aesthetic & Art — Mary
excerpt: Mary is one of the enemies the Lambs face in arc 1/episode 1. A clone, grown in a vat and keyed to be an assassin, she took the place of an aristocratic young lady of the same name while that young lady was supposed to be at a high-end

title: Ward: Art Direction (character notes) — Abstract elements:
excerpt: Stark contrasts of black/white, not necessarily light/dark. Starkness and austerity are a general rule, set aside from elegance and femininity. If she has fifty dollars, she’ll spend it on one thing that looks great and fits her perfectly,

title: Ward: Art Direction (character notes) — Personality
excerpt: Ashley is all about the theater of the cape world. Put her in a mundane situation like when she’s sweeping the floors (if her hands are in a state that allows it) and she’ll wear an expression like she’s done with this shit, even if she’s

title: Ward: Art Direction (character notes) — Physical details
excerpt: Ashley is 5’6”, 167cm, but will wear shoes that give her a bit of extra height. Her physical frame is skinny, her chest is small (and she doesn’t care). She’s Caucasian-Scandanavian with very straight, fine hair that’s pale blonde even

title: Ward: Art Direction (character notes) — Power
excerpt: Distortion that makes the light darken, like how light might pass through a glass and cast a shadow on the wall. Blurring. Bleaching light and deepening shadow. It rips, tears, overlaps itself and includes veins of raw darkness. There

title: Ward: Art Direction (character notes) — Byron & Tristan - Capricorn
excerpt:
Capricorn

Age: 17

Ethnicity: Hispanic (Mexican-American parents) (t) - Black hair, short on sides, magenta-red on top, curled waves (b) - Black hair, medium long, wavy, brushed black. Height: 5’9” / 178cm
